Compétence Interprétative

20%

Compétence Interprétative

Compétence Interprétative — Reading Comprehension
Méthode pour lire et répondre aux questions sur un texte. Représente 30% de l'examen.
1. Lire le titre et survoler le texte (30 sec)
2. Lire les questions AVANT le texte
3. Lire le texte en soulignant les informations clés
4. Répondre en PHRASES COMPLÈTES (sujet + verbe + complément)
5. Toujours reformuler avec vos propres mots, ne jamais copier mot pour mot
What is the main idea of the text?
According to the text, what are...
How / Why / Where / When / Who...
True or False — justify your answer
Summarize the text in X complete sentences
What suitable title would you suggest?
Santé / Health
Malaria & mosquitoes
Vaccination & smallpox
Health awareness / smoking / alcohol
Transportation in Haiti
Personnages célèbres
Nelson Mandela
Marie & Pierre Curie
Ibrahim Cisse (immigrant)
Société
School dropout (Morocco)
Refugees
Cellular phones
Organic food
Sport & children
Climate change
Language barrier
Récits / Stories
Pedro Ramirez (fire)
Mr. Yamamoto (briefcase — In good faith)
Warly at the airport
What is malaria?
It's a parasite.
Malaria is a disease caused by a parasite that invades red blood cells and has a three-stage life cycle. It is transmitted through mosquito bites.
Summarize in 4 complete sentences
Phrase 1 : Le sujet principal + l'idée centrale
Phrase 2 : Les causes ou le contexte
Phrase 3 : Les conséquences ou les solutions mentionnées
Phrase 4 : La conclusion ou le message final
Les Temps Verbaux — Verb Tenses
Les temps les plus testés au Bac haïtien
Simple Present
Subject + V (base) / He/She/It + V+s
Habitudes, vérités générales
She studies every day.
The earth revolves around the sun.
He doesn't like vegetables.
always, usually, often, sometimes, never, every day
Present Continuous
Subject + am/is/are + V-ing
Action en cours au moment où on parle
She is studying right now.
They are building a new bridge.
At the moment, we are studying poems.
now, right now, at the moment, these days, currently
Simple Past
Subject + V-ed / irregular verb
Action terminée dans le passé
He went to the market yesterday.
She lived in Paris for three years.
They found a homeless man on the ship.
yesterday, last week/year, ago, in 1990, once
Past Continuous
Subject + was/were + V-ing
Action en cours dans le passé (souvent interrompue)
She was lying on her bed thinking about the party.
While Pedro was gathering documents, Luisa grabbed the photos.
while, when, at that moment
Present Perfect
Subject + have/has + V-pp
Action passée avec lien au présent, expérience, résultat
Thomas has been planning his vacation for several months.
She has never traveled abroad.
I haven't seen him since Christmas.
since, for, already, yet, just, ever, never, recently
Past Perfect
Subject + had + V-pp
Action qui s'est passée AVANT une autre action passée
By the time I got there, Bill had already left.
She had never expected to get the job.
I thought you had finished reading it.
by the time, already, before, after, when (+ passé)
Future (will)
Subject + will + V (base)
Prédiction, décision spontanée
I will give you a call as soon as I get your number.
She will become a dancer one day.
We will have built the bridge by the end of the year.
tomorrow, soon, next week/year, in the future
Conditional — If Clauses
If + present → will + V (Si c'est possible)
If + simple past → would + V (Si c'était possible)
If + past perfect → would have + V-pp (Si ça avait été possible)
If you put your money in your wallet, you wouldn't lose it. (Type 2)
If I get this position, I will be very happy! (Type 1)
You would have won if you had taken part in the contest. (Type 3)
If I were you, I would explain what happened. (Type 2)
Identifiez le type : présent→Type 1, passé simple→Type 2, past perfect→Type 3
Bac 2022 SVT/SES/SMP
Thomas _____ his vacation for several months.
a) planning
b) has been planning
c) is planning
d) been planning for
b) has been planning
'for several months' → Present Perfect Continuous
Bac 2019 SVT/SES/SMP
By the time I got there, Bill _____.
a) had already left
b) left
c) is leaving
a) had already left
'By the time I got' → Past Perfect pour l'action antérieure
Bac 2022
If I _____ you, I would explain what happened. (be)
were
Type 2 Conditional: If + past simple (were pour tous les sujets avec 'if')
Tag Questions
Questions ajoutées en fin de phrase pour chercher confirmation. Très testées au Bac.
Si la phrase est AFFIRMATIVE → tag NÉGATIF / Si la phrase est NÉGATIVE → tag POSITIF. Le tag reprend l'auxiliaire de la phrase principale.
Phrase affirmative + auxiliaire négatif + pronom ?
Phrase négative + auxiliaire positif + pronom ?
Roberto wasn't in class today, ___?
was he
wasn't = négatif → tag positif was
You didn't damage my car, ___?
did you
didn't = négatif → tag positif did
Everyone brought a dish, ___?
didn't they
affirmatif → tag négatif didn't
She has never been there, ___?
has she
has never (négatif) → tag positif has
You haven't seen Paul today, ___?
have you
haven't = négatif → tag positif have
I am right, ___?
aren't I
Let's go, ___?
shall we
Everyone enjoyed, ___?
didn't they (everyone = they)
Voix Passive & Active — Passive/Active Voice
Transformation entre voix active et passive. Testée tous les ans.
Object of active → Subject + to be (conjugué) + Past Participle (+ by + agent)
Simple Present
She delivers the mail.
The mail is delivered (by her).
Simple Past
An expert valued the painting.
The painting was valued by an expert.
Present Perfect
They have built a new school.
A new school has been built.
Future (will)
They will build the bridge.
The bridge will be built.
Present Continuous
Organizers are making preparations.
Preparations are being made by the organizers.
Modal (can/must/should)
Sue can't pay the bill.
The bill can't be paid by Sue.
Bac 2022
I made this pie with onions and cheese.
This pie was made (by me) with onions and cheese.
Bac 2022
They will have built the new bridge by the end of the year.
The new bridge will have been built by the end of the year.
Bac 2019
Her excuse may not be believed by her parents.
Her parents may not believe her excuse.
Bac 2019
Hot water is provided by the hotel 24 hours a day.
The hotel provides hot water 24 hours a day.
Pour passer du passif à l'actif: 'by + agent' devient le sujet. L'objet principal du passif devient l'objet de l'actif.
Discours Indirect — Reported Speech
Transformer du discours direct en discours indirect. Testé chaque année.
Simple Present
Simple Past
Present Continuous
Past Continuous
Present Perfect
Past Perfect
Simple Past
Past Perfect
will
would
can
could
may
might
I → he/she
we → they
my → his/her
you → he/she/they (selon contexte)
now → then
today → that day
yesterday → the day before
tomorrow → the next day / the following day
here → there
this → that
"We are preparing a cultural event," the organizers said.
The organizers said that they were preparing a cultural event.
He said, "You can learn a lot from people."
He said that we could learn a lot from people.
She said, "Ibrahim saw cultural differences as opportunities."
She said that Ibrahim had seen cultural differences as opportunities.
People say, "We are the same."
People say that they are the same. (say → say / no backshift si present)
Déclaration (said/told)
said that + phrase
She said that she was tired.
Question (asked)
asked if/whether + phrase (oui/non) ou asked + wh-word + phrase
He asked if I had seen the movie.
Ordre (told/asked to)
told/asked + person + to + infinitif
She told him to be quiet.
Comparatifs & Superlatifs
Formes comparative et superlative des adjectifs. Testées chaque année.
Adjectifs courts (1 syllabe)
adj + -er + than
the + adj + -est
short → shorter than → the shortest
big → bigger than → the biggest
high → higher than → the highest
Adjectifs longs (2+ syllabes)
more + adj + than
the most + adj
comfortable → more comfortable than → the most comfortable
expensive → less expensive than → the least expensive
Irréguliers
good
better
the best
bad
worse
the worst
far
farther/further
the farthest/furthest
little
less
the least
much/many
more
the most
as + adj + as → My portion was as large as Bob's.
Bac 2019
Benny's apartment is _____ (high) of all three.
the highest
Bac 2019
Abel's lease is _____ (short) than mine.
shorter
Bac 2022
The game will certainly be much (exciting) _____ than last year.
more exciting
Bac 2022
That was by far (bad) _____ hospital ever built.
the worst
Gérondif & Infinitif — Gerund vs Infinitive
Après certains verbes, on utilise le gérondif (V-ing) ou l'infinitif (to + V). Testé régulièrement.
enjoy, avoid, mind, suggest, consider, keep, finish, stop, admit, deny, miss, recommend, practice, look forward to, be used to, be afraid of, be tired of, plan on, insist on
Edmond enjoys hiking and fishing. (NOT to fish)
The boss is tired of listening to complaints.
She is looking forward to meeting you.
Many candidates aren't used to taking exams.
He's afraid of going by plane.
want, need, decide, hope, plan, agree, refuse, promise, manage, try, forget, remember (future), would like, would rather, used to, tell someone, ask someone, advise someone
The network decided to hire two new employees.
She told him to be quiet.
I would like to apologize for interrupting you.
I used to go fishing when I was younger.
Did you remember to pack your swimsuit?
stop
Stop smoking → arrêter de fumer (définitif)
Stop to smoke → s'arrêter POUR fumer
remember
I remember meeting her → se souvenir d'avoir fait
Remember to call her → ne pas oublier de faire
try
Try cleaning the DVD → essayer de (tenter)
Try to be on time → essayer de (effort)
Bac 2019
I would like (apologize) ___ for (interrupt) ___ you.
to apologize / interrupting
Bac 2019
Everybody looks forward to (be) ___ on vacation.
being
Bac 2022
Did you remember ___ your swimsuit? (pack)
to pack
Bac 2022
He's afraid of ___ by plane. (go)
going
Adjectifs en -ED et -ING
Confusion fréquente au Bac. La règle est simple mais piège souvent.
Décrit quelque chose qui CAUSE la sensation (le sujet actif)
Décrit comment quelqu'un SE SENT (la personne affectée)
interest
The movie is interesting. (le film donne de l'intérêt)
I am interested in the movie. (je ressens l'intérêt)
bore
The lesson is boring. (la leçon ennuie)
Jack is bored with school. (il ressent l'ennui)
disappoint
The result was disappointing. (déçoit)
I was disappointed by the result. (je suis déçu)
embarrass
It's embarrassing to ask for money.
She felt embarrassed.
surprise
The news was surprising.
I was surprised when I got the job.
fascinate
The play was fascinating.
We were all fascinated by the play.
amaze
It was an amazing experience.
I was amazed when it was offered to me.
Si c'est UNE PERSONNE qui ressent → -ED. Si c'est UNE CHOSE qui provoque → -ING.
Prépositions de Temps — For, Since, Ago, In, On, At
Erreur très fréquente au Bac. Maîtrisez ces 6 prépositions.
FOR
Durée (combien de temps)
for three years
for several months
for a long time
for more than a century
Women have been fighting ___ more than a century. → FOR
SINCE
Point de départ dans le passé (depuis quand)
since 1990
since Monday
since the day he met you
since last Christmas
I haven't seen him ___ September. → SINCE
AGO
Temps écoulé depuis (il y a...)
three years ago
a long time ago
two months ago
A long time ___, Haiti was the Pearl of the Caribbean. → AGO
IN
Mois, années, siècles, parties du jour (morning/afternoon/evening)
in the morning
in 1990
in July
in the 18th century
What do you usually do ___ the morning? → IN
ON
Jours spécifiques, dates
on Monday
on Monday afternoon
on July 18
on that day
I have got an interview ___ Monday afternoon. → ON
AT
Heures précises, moments spécifiques (night/noon/midnight/the weekend)
at 6:00 pm
at night
at noon
at midnight
Generally, ___ night, diurnal birds don't fly. → AT
FOR + durée (3 years) / SINCE + moment précis (1990, Monday)
Prépositions de Lieu & Autres Prépositions Courantes
Prépositions après certains verbes. Testées régulièrement.
lied about
Evens thinks Margaret lied ABOUT her age.
paid for
The museum paid $5000 FOR that drawing.
insist on
The boss didn't want to insist ON taking us to lunch.
quarreled over
Robert and Jane almost quarreled OVER where to plant the roses.
blame for
She blamed him FOR not saying anything.
look into
Ann looked INTO the mirror.
look at
She looked AT the painting.
interested in
Are you interested IN soccer?
tired of
The boss is tired OF listening to complaints.
afraid of
He is afraid OF flying.
fond of
I am very fond OF her.
Propositions Relatives — Relative Clauses
Connecter deux phrases avec who, whose, whom, which, when, where.
WHO
Personnes — sujet
Do you know the doctor WHO wrote that book?
WHOSE
Possession (personnes ou choses)
Do you know the doctor WHOSE husband wrote a book?
WHOM
Personnes — objet (formel)
Do you know the patient WHOM I saw?
WHICH
Choses ou animaux
The ball WHICH is on the table is mine.
WHEN
Temps
1966 was the year WHEN England won the World Cup.
WHERE
Lieu
This is the village WHERE my grandfather was born.
Bac 2025
This is the village.
My great-grandfather was born there.
This is the village WHERE my great-grandfather was born.
Bac 2022
Do you know the doctor?
Her husband wrote a book.
Do you know the doctor WHOSE husband wrote a book?
Bac 2022
The nurses were busy.
We saw them.
The nurses WHO we saw were busy. / We saw the nurses WHO were busy.
Mots Composés & Préfixes — Under / Over
Under = trop peu / Over = trop. Testé surtout en série LLA.
underslept
pas assez dormi
My sister didn't hear the alarm, so she underslept.
underpaid
sous-payé
The workers say they are underpaid.
underdeveloped
sous-développé
Rich nations should give more aid to underdeveloped countries.
underdone
pas assez cuit
The steak is underdone, it needs more cooking.
overcrowded
trop plein de monde
The room was overcrowded.
overslept
trop dormi / dormir trop tard
I overslept and missed the bus.
overweight
surpoids
Moderate exercise helps prevent overweight.
Vocabulaire Essentiel au Bac
Mots et expressions récurrents dans les examens haïtiens
parasite
an organism that lives and feeds on another organism
vaccine / vaccination
a substance injected to protect against disease
immune system
the body's defense against diseases
eradication
the complete elimination of a disease
pesticide / insecticide
chemical used to kill insects
psychiatrist
a medical doctor who treats mental health problems (can prescribe medication)
psychologist
PhD, helps with mental/family issues but CANNOT prescribe medication
chiropractor
licensed alternative medicine professional
respiratory
related to breathing
renewable energy
energy from sources that are naturally replenished (sun, wind, water)
carbon neutral
not adding net carbon dioxide to the atmosphere
ecosystem
the relationship between living things and their environment
conservation efforts
measures to protect and restore the natural world
global warming
the gradual increase in temperature on Earth
food chain
a group of organisms that feed off of each other
wildlife
plants and animals living in a natural environment
passport
identity document for international travel
customs
where documents are checked at the airport before departure
itinerary
the plan or schedule of a trip
destination
the final place you are going to
sightseeing
visiting famous or interesting places as a tourist
very cold
freezing
very slim
skinny
very tired
exhausted
very huge
colossal
very small
tiny
very important
crucial
very happy
delighted
very sad
depressed
very impressive
extraordinary
very intelligent
genius
amnesia
a condition that causes people to lose their memory
castle
a solid structure usually fortified against attack
flood
a large amount of water covering an area that is usually dry
crutches
supports used when someone has a broken leg
editorial
an article in a newspaper expressing the opinion of the editor
illiteracy
the inability to read or write
addiction
dependency on something (computers, drugs, etc.)
flight
a journey by airplane
interview
a formal meeting for a job or information
Pronoms Indéfinis — Whoever, Whatever, Wherever, However, Whichever
Pronoms composés avec -ever. Testés surtout en SVT/SES/SMP.
WHOEVER
quiconque / n'importe qui
You can invite whoever you want — it's your party.
WHATEVER
quoi que / n'importe quoi
Keep cool, whatever stupid things he says.
WHEREVER
où que / n'importe où
Sit down wherever you like.
HOWEVER
de quelque façon que / quel que soit le degré
Keep calm, however stupid life can be.
WHICHEVER
lequel que / n'importe lequel
Borrow whichever dictionary you want.
Compétence Pragmatique — Problem-Solving Situations
Partie III de l'examen. Vous devez résoudre un dilemme moral ou pratique. Représente 20% de la note.
Introduction : Présenter la situation en une phrase
Corps (3-5 phrases) : Expliquer votre décision et VOS RAISONS
Conclusion : Affirmer votre position finale
Bac 2022 LLA (Atlanta)
On a sinking ship, you must choose 3 out of 6 passengers: nurse, priest, your mother, football player, survival expert, 9-year-old boy.
Choisir selon les compétences utiles à la survie (infirmière + expert survie) + facteur émotionnel/moral (mère ou enfant). Justifier avec des arguments logiques.
Bac 2019 SVT/SES/SMP
You accidentally killed a pedestrian. A crying woman believes she did it. No witnesses. What do you do?
Dilemme moral — la vérité vs protéger quelqu'un. Argument pour dire la vérité : justice, conscience, ne pas laisser souffrir innocemment.
Bac 2022 SVT/SES/SMP
A classmate is too physically close to you. How do you react without embarrassing them?
Réponse polie, directe mais respectueuse. Expliquer le malaise calmement en privé.
Bac 2019 LLA
Warly at airport — miss her flight to help a fallen elderly man, or leave him?
Argument humaniste : aider l'homme en difficulté, appeler les secours, expliquer la situation à sa famille. La vie humaine prime sur un événement familial.
In my opinion / From my point of view...
I strongly believe that...
I would not hesitate to...
The right thing to do is...
On one hand... On the other hand...
Even though it is difficult, I would...
I would rather... than...
My conscience would not allow me to...
The most logical solution would be to...
Compétence Discursive — Written Production & Essay
Partie IV de l'examen. Rédiger un essai de 10-12 lignes sur un sujet au choix. Représente 20% de la note.
Present the topic + your position (2-3 phrases)
2-3 arguments with examples/justifications (6-7 phrases)
Restate your position + opening thought (2 phrases)
La vie en communauté / community life
Le sport — ses bienfaits / sport benefits
Les langues étrangères / foreign languages
L'éducation et son importance / importance of education
La technologie (téléphone portable) / technology
La protection de l'environnement / environment
Le changement climatique / climate change
La carrière après le Bac / career after high school
L'immigration / la diaspora haïtienne
Le football en Haïti / sport in Haiti
Internet et la personnalité / internet and personality
L'alimentation biologique / organic food
Nowadays / In today's world...
It is widely acknowledged that...
The question of... has been a major concern.
Many people argue that...
First of all / To begin with...
Furthermore / Moreover / In addition...
Another important point is that...
For example / For instance...
However / On the other hand...
Although / Even though...
Despite / In spite of...
In conclusion / To sum up...
Taking everything into account...
All things considered...
Therefore, I strongly believe that...
What do you think should be done to improve football in Haiti?
Football is undeniably one of the most popular sports in Haiti, yet the country lacks the infrastructure and support needed to develop world-class talent. First of all, the government should invest in building proper football fields and training centers across the country. Many talented young Haitians never get the chance to develop their skills due to the lack of facilities. Furthermore, establishing structured youth academies would give promising players professional training from an early age. For example, countries like Jamaica and Trinidad have improved significantly by creating football academies. In addition, local leagues should be better organized and supported financially so that Haitian players can compete at a high level domestically. In conclusion, with proper investment in infrastructure, youth programs, and league organization, Haiti could truly unlock its football potential and produce players who compete on the world stage.
Sujets Complets Type Bac avec Corrigés
Entraînement final basé sur les vrais examens du MENFP
Adapté — Bac SVT/SES/SMP type 2022
The Importance of Sport for Children's Health

Sport helps children to develop both physically and mentally. Physical activity should be part of our daily life. Practicing any kind of sport from an early age helps children in many ways: group activities improve interaction and social integration. Sports also improve mental levels and promote a healthy lifestyle in adulthood. If we talk at a psychological level, sports help children strengthen their social self-esteem and learn to respect rules. Moderate daily exercise also helps prevent overweight and obesity.
State the psychological benefits of doing sports activities.
According to the text, sports activities help children strengthen their social self-esteem and allow them to understand the importance of having and respecting rules.
Why does the inclusion of sport become compulsory in the family routine?
The inclusion of sport has become almost mandatory in family routines because of the growing percentage of children and young people with obesity and overweight in the Western world.
Summarize the text in three complete sentences.
Sport is essential for children's physical and mental development. Group activities improve social skills and self-esteem, while individual sports help develop concentration. Daily physical exercise also helps prevent obesity and promotes healthy habits that last into adulthood.
Thomas _____ his vacation for several months. (a) planning (b) has been planning (c) is planning
b) has been planning
Signal word 'for several months' → Present Perfect (Continuous)
Roberto wasn't in class today, _____?
was he
wasn't = négatif → tag positif 'was'; pronom = he
I was (disappointing/disappointed) by the movie.
disappointed
Je RESSENS la déception → -ed
Turn to passive: I made this pie with onions and cheese.
This pie was made (by me) with onions and cheese.
You are at a birthday party and finally it's your turn at the serving table. Suddenly you need to go to the bathroom. The line is still long. What do you do?
In this situation, I would politely ask the person behind me to hold my place in line while I quickly use the restroom. I would explain my situation briefly and thank them in advance. If they agree, I would rush and return as quickly as possible. This way, I would not lose my turn entirely and would avoid any conflict or embarrassment. I believe that a simple, respectful request is the best solution in such an unexpected situation.
What do you think should be done to improve football in Haiti?
Intro: Importance du football en Haïti + problème actuel
Arg 1: Investissement en infrastructures (terrains, académies)
Arg 2: Développement des ligues locales
Arg 3: Formation des entraîneurs / soutien gouvernemental
Conclusion: Le potentiel existe, il faut l'investissement
Quiz Final — Questions Tirées des Vrais Examens 2019-2025
Testez vos connaissances avec des questions officielles
Q1
Temps verbal
Thomas _____ his vacation for several months. (planning / has been planning / is planning / been planning for)
has been planning
for several months → Present Perfect Continuous (Bac 2022)
Q2
Tag question
You didn't damage my car, _____?
did you
Phrase négative → tag positif / auxiliaire 'did' (Bac 2022)
Q3
Adjectif -ed/-ing
We were all _____ by the play. (fascinating / fascinated)
fascinated
Nous RESSENTONS → -ed (Bac 2022)
Q4
Discours indirect
"We are preparing a cultural event," the organizers said. → Reported speech
The organizers said that they were preparing a cultural event.
are → were / we → they (Bac 2025)
Q5
Voix passive
Turn to passive: Everyone considers her to be the best student.
She is considered to be the best student (by everyone).
Everyone → subject omitted / considers → is considered (Bac 2022)
Q6
Conditionnel
If I _____ you, I would explain what happened. (be)
were
Type 2 Conditional: If + past simple (were pour tous sujets) (Bac 2022)
Q7
Préposition
Women have been fighting to achieve gender equality _____ more than a century.
for
Durée → FOR (Bac 2020)
Q8
Gérondif/Infinitif
Everybody looks forward to _____ on vacation. (be)
being
look forward to + gérondif (Bac 2019)
Q9
Comparatif
That was by far (bad) _____ hospital ever built.
the worst
Superlatif de 'bad' (irrégulier) = the worst (Bac 2022)
Q10
Vocabulaire
Which professional can prescribe medication for psychological problems?
A psychiatrist
Psychiatrists are medical doctors. Psychologists cannot prescribe. (Bac 2019)
Q11
Pronom relatif
1966 was the year _____ England won the football World Cup.
when
WHEN pour le temps (Bac 2022)
Q12
Préfixe
There were too many people in the room. It was _____crowded.
over
over = trop → overcrowded (Bac 2022)
Q13
Vocabulaire fort
What is the strong equivalent of 'very cold'?
freezing
Tableau d'intensificateurs (Bac 2022 Cleveland)
Q14
Pronom indéfini
You can invite _____ you want — It's your party. (whoever/whatever/wherever)
whoever
whoever = n'importe qui (personne) (Bac 2022)
Q15
Voix active
Turn to active: Hot water is provided by the hotel 24 hours a day.
The hotel provides hot water 24 hours a day.
by the hotel → sujet / is provided → provides (Bac 2019)
1. Les temps verbaux (present perfect, past perfect, conditionnels)
2. Tag questions (affirmatif→négatif, négatif→positif)
3. Voix passive/active (tous les temps)
4. Discours indirect (changement de temps et pronoms)
5. Comparatifs et superlatifs (réguliers et irréguliers)
6. Gérondif vs infinitif (enjoy+ing / decide+to)
7. Adjectifs -ed vs -ing (disappointed vs disappointing)
Lire les questions AVANT le texte
Répondre en PHRASES COMPLÈTES (obligatoire)
Ne pas copier mot pour mot — reformuler
Résumé = 3-5 phrases couvrant intro, développement, conclusion
Toujours faire un PLAN avant d'écrire
Minimum 10-12 lignes demandées
Utiliser des connecteurs (First / Furthermore / However / In conclusion)
Donner des exemples concrets
Vérifier la grammaire à la fin
Santé : parasite, vaccine, immune system, psychiatrist vs psychologist
Environnement : renewable energy, carbon neutral, ecosystem, global warming
Mots forts : freezing, exhausted, tiny, crucial, delighted, depressed
Voyage : passport, customs, itinerary, destination, sightseeing
